item.description: She was the best person I knew. With a wonderful sense of humor.
item.dc:creator: bib21693

item.title: Scars.
item.dc:creator: infallibledegenerate

item.title: The end of a very long search.
item.description: There was a song on a random VHS we rented back in the early 80's. Years went by and I couldn't remember the name of the film (I was a kid). I thought I found it once in the 90's, playing in a shop in Dublin, but it was on a ripped CD with no track listing. Figured it would eventually pop up again one day, but was always a bit sad it was so elusive. Finally, Blue In Green by Miles Davis played on the radio this morning. I leapt out of bed like a madman, to Shazam the shit out of it, ending a tortuous 40 year search. Happy New Year to you all! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TLDflhhdPCg&ab_channel=MilesDavisVEVO Edit: Ooh, this has gone well. If you can help anyone in the comments find their lost 'thing' it would be great to spread the joy. Thanks to @AliceHope78 for suggesting a subreddit called 'tip of the tongue', it might bear fruit if you're looking for something difficult.
item.dc:creator: TedBuckley

item.dc:creator: rockyandchloe

item.title: Happy New Year, Imgur, and thanks for all the fish!
item.description:
(artist: nekosnicker) To all of you who shitpost, comment, and lurk, happy new year and thank you from me and Story. This year had its ups and downs at Elbow House, more recently being a rapid series of downs that you folks helped me navigate. I thought the breakdown of my car would be the worst of it, but then my little furgoober got sick and everything felt small by comparison.
Before this past November, I'd never experienced an animal emergency where I didn't have a dozen other coworkers, supervisor, and on-call veterinarian to lean on. Raising horses on a farm is nothing like raising a pet alone. I learned a lot, real quick, and not in the way I wanted. Watching my cat go from active and attention seeking to lethargic and practically immobile in the span of a few hours was one of the worst fears I've experienced in a long, long time. I lose track of how many of you reached out to talk to me while Story was hospitalized two hours away, and kept in touch after she got back home. You people kept me sane so I could keep focused on caring for my furball.
Her recovery was slow, which isn't a pace I usually operate at. Everything I had going on - projects, restorations, socializing, recreation - got put on hold while I worked with Story on taking her medication, adjusting the house to suit her restrictions, and just being around when she wanted company.
The morning I woke up to this Ring notification, I finally let myself relax a little. Something as simple as seeing my cat drinking water without being coaxed into it was huge. It's like holding your breath snd finally being allowed to exhale.
We made slow adjustments to accommodate her arthritis, made the living room *her* room, and anything else I could think of to make her feel comfortable.
It took over a month of trying one prescription food after another, trying out different ways to serve it, different bowls, different locations, all while Story's stomach was upset from the sudden diet change to dissolve the stones in her bladder and clear out her crystals. She wasn't happy about the checkup, but she bounced back quick.
Now that her stones are gone, she was able to reacclimate to her original diet until her tummy settled and is now transitioning slowly onto her prescription food.
It took a lot of time, stress, and minor emotional breakdowns, but Story's back to her spunky 10 year old self!
It goes without saying, but thank you to everyone who helped pull my fat out of the fire over the past few months. Between the car repair bills and Story's medical expenses, I'd be deep in debt were it not for the kindness of so many friends and strangers. You guys were the difference that kept a financial gutshot from turning into a financial landmine.
But more importantly, you folks took away a massive weight of stress at a time when I was having trouble enough with seeing my baby girl in pain. Story's doing much better now, thanks in part to all of you who kept me sane during the holiday season. 2023 was a rough one, and I have high hopes that 2024 will be a little bit better with this fuzzy screwball keeping me company.
(artist: inowiseei) Thank you to all of you who tolerate my weird brand of shitposting, the occasional antique restorations, and glimpses of the real me. Story thanks you too. I'll see you weirdos next year. <3
item.dc:creator: ElbowDeepInAHorse

item.title: Haute Dog Archives #40 - New York
item.description:
Good evening, fellow imgurians, and welcome to the final chapter. The end of the Haute Dog Archives has arrived! It has been an arduous journey these last 9 months, and now it is time to lay it all to rest. For those just dropping in, this is part two of a double feature! Yesterday, I made a Seattle Dog, complete with homemade bagel buns. It was a most impressive contender, nearing the top of the rankings, but not quite number one. With only a few hours before the new year, let's finish this with a (firework) bang!
Ah yes, the New York. As I said way back in the very first archive, the hot dog's roots came from Frankfurt, Germany, and spread to the United States as early as the mid-1800s. Specifically, the earliest reports of the hot dog were in New York! How fitting that I started with Germany and ended it here, eh? The stand-out ingredient this time around is the onion sauce, a blend of caramelized onions, tomatoes, and seasonings. It also employs an all-beef wiener, and a spicy brown variant of mustard.
I promised I would end these posts strong, so I'll give this everything I've got. Now then, COMMENCE SAND WITCHCRAFT!!
Starting with the onion sauce. Because I wasn't too sure how to approach this, I snooped around on the internet for a reliable recipe. The fellow who wrote out the recipe I'll be using is a Bronx-born Queens resident. Short of the sauce screaming "I'm saucin' here!" at me, it doesn't get more New York than that, eh?
To begin, I need to slice up this onion. I was smart enough to put on some safety glasses to protect my eyes before beginning, so maybe this time won't end in tears.
Y'know, I probably don't need to be using the entire onion. I think I can make do with just half.
Okay, so the safety glasses are BUTT! All they did was trap the onion vapours more closely into my eyes AMPLIFYING THE PAIN!! ERJVNORB[WRGBJGWMEV @!32rmF32oMVERPMI3OM[!!!!!111!!!!
I am in a mood most foul!
Next, in an inadvisably small skillet, I'm adding 1/2 tsp of vegetable oil,
And in go the onions! Thankfully, I don't have to sauté them for nearly as long as I did yesterday. I still need them to soften up a bit though.
Okay, they're looking soft enough now. The next step was to add 1/4 cup of water and 1 tbsp of ketchup into the mix. At first I was surprised about the ketchup, but all things considered, it is tomato paste and vinegar in one. That's efficiency!
It's not looking too great, but I shall trust the process!!
My trust has been rewarded! Huzzah!
The recipe also calls for a pinch of salt and cinnamon, so I added accordingly!
Look at that, I made slightly more that yesterday...
Alright, let's move on to the sauerkraut. Normally, I'd just serve from the jar, but I'm gonna try something a little different. See, I've realized through these archives that I'm not the biggest fan of sauerkraut, so without altering its taste too much, I'm gonna see if I can improve it with some basic seasoning. First things first, I'm draining the sauerkraut and patting dry with some paper towel...
Next, I'm gonna melt some butter in the pan. Ideally I'd be using lard, but I don't have lard, so I'm using butter.
In goes the sauerkraut...
Followed by a sprinkling of salt and pepper! This should keep the overall crunch and taste of the sauerkraut, but hopefully reduce the tanginess a bit, as that seems to be a flavour I struggle with.
You may have noticed I switched to my left burner for the sauerkraut. That's because I've been busy using the right burner to boil water for the beef wieners! MULTI-TASKING! MWAHAHAHAHA!
I'm really hoping they cook through properly...
HUZZAH! VICTORY!
And while I'm at it, I might as well reappropriate that boiling water to steaming the buns! See? I've become a multitasking machine!!
Huh, you can't really see the steam in this picture. You can see the tongs, however, and I assure you I clicked them twice before usage.
And that's everything! I think we're good for assembly!
Now, it wasn't mentioned in the infographic, but since these are traditionally hotdogs from a street vendor, I'm gonna assume there's butter on the bun. No, I'm not addicted to butter.
Next, our boiled beef wiener. A bit of a downsize from the bratwurst yesterday, but I imagine it'll still taste nice.
Next, a slathering of brown spicy mustard!
Followed by the onion sauce,
And finally, some dry seasoned sauerkraut! Now, where did I put that tin foil...
VOILA!
So, how did it taste? What a way to end the year. This one was a proper show stopper! There isn't too much I can say about the bun and wieners that I haven't said already, so let's skip to the toppings. I'm pleased to say the sauerkraut actually works really well here! I think it's mainly because of drying it of its juices, but the salt and pepper is a tried and true seasoning combo that can make anything taste more balanced. It doesn't feel like a sour punch to the taste buds, and it still manages to retain that much needed crunch! I'm also quite pleased with the mustard, which unlike yellow mustard, is more subtle in its own tanginess. It's very similar to standard dijon, but the added spiciness does make the taste more appealing. The star of the show is of course the onion sauce. What a fantastic topping it is! It manages to be both sweet and umami in a way that complements both the wiener and the other toppings. It's a lot better than the onions from the Seattle, most certainly. All in all, this was a delight! And what a great way to end the year! So, the final rankings? 1. Sonora https://imgur.com/gallery/4RCzqu5 2. Michigan https://imgur.com/gallery/o9lsWuM 3. Seattle https://imgur.com/gallery/w9E6j7k 4. Denver https://imgur.com/gallery/uLARvXN 5. Memphis https://imgur.com/gallery/gLNtaJn 6. New York 7. Carolina https://imgur.com/gallery/BlcEbrJ 8. Chicago https://imgur.com/gallery/NzZuk7B 9. Colombia https://imgur.com/gallery/GldUSzT 10. Guatemala https://imgur.com/gallery/wd0db7C 11. Tijuana https://imgur.com/gallery/ZEbPQyv 12. Vietnam https://imgur.com/gallery/q2n5JT8 13. Doyer https://imgur.com/gallery/FTmAZTj 14. Montreal https://imgur.com/gallery/AR3u8Mc 15. Korea https://imgur.com/gallery/uRT2yqw 16. Denmark https://imgur.com/gallery/nf3Erv3 17. Philippines https://imgur.com/gallery/e6kVaTW 18. Italian https://imgur.com/gallery/hECHrK5 19. Potato Dog https://imgur.com/gallery/3jf1XIL 20. Brazil https://imgur.com/gallery/q01nHeX 21. Georgia https://imgur.com/gallery/Fb6rf3p 22. Peru https://imgur.com/gallery/ZczSFfg 23. Dodger https://imgur.com/gallery/ibcO92R 24. Idaho https://imgur.com/gallery/l0vew85 25. Chile https://imgur.com/gallery/9bGIx29 26. Bagel Dog https://imgur.com/gallery/lWXbTAZ 27. Baltimore https://imgur.com/gallery/FtNezbl 28. Coney https://imgur.com/gallery/YlUBBcN 29. Sweden https://imgur.com/gallery/y3RJrHt 30. Amsterdam https://imgur.com/gallery/AyNixPR 31. Germany https://imgur.com/gallery/fuExz2W 32. Kansas City https://imgur.com/gallery/lbpUcxN 33. Hawaii https://imgur.com/gallery/xoIkWGz 34. Argentina https://imgur.com/gallery/0FVKZ1q 35. France https://imgur.com/gallery/gpvvk9f 36. Norway https://imgur.com/gallery/vMf6zEC 37. China https://imgur.com/gallery/poqiM5o 38. Czech Republic https://imgur.com/gallery/hwsEHhd 39. Japan https://imgur.com/gallery/7rfYv6c 40. Cleveland https://imgur.com/gallery/flUDezM Still not as good as the unsalted buttered crusts, though... -- That's it, then. At long last, the Haute Dog Archives are over. I am still an Imgurian of my word (where the hell's my 'op delivered' badge?). It still blows my mind that these posts reach the front page, and that so many people ask to be apart of the tag list to see the next sandwich/hot dog! To be completely honest, this year wasn't the best for me. I had been going through more than a couple of hiccups in my life, and it's not exaggerating to say that I had fallen into something of a depression and didn't even realize it. I went into April thinking I'd do a little April Fools gag as a joke for those who read the Sand Witch Chronicles, but I never imagined so many would unironically want me to continue. So, I moved forward, on a journey of pork, beef, and a considerably lower amount of kewpie mayo. I did many things I never thought I could, like baking, and making chili! I learned more about myself, and slowly regained the enthusiasm I once had one odd year ago. I appreciate you all checking up on me when I got sick, your willingness to send ingredients my way, and I am truly honoured each and every time you request to donate money. It wasn't something I had ever planned on because it felt so awkward, but I have decided to heed your suggestions and open a ko-fi account. If you thought my posts were worth a tip of appreciation, feel free and thank you! https://ko-fi.com/misterswissroll None of this would ever have been possible without the support (and advice) from every single one of you guys. It is always my hope that my posts can pick you guys up in the same way. Here's to a new year for all of us! Thank you so much for reading, imgur. Happy New Years!!
item.dc:creator: MrSwissroll

item.title: I’ll never forgive the people who tried to gaslight us into thinking that NFTs make sense
item.dc:creator: J2UDIGI
